question_text: A kettle uses 1000 J of electrical energy to heat water. Some of this energy heats the water (useful) and some heats the kettle itself (wasted). Which statement is correct?

options.label: A
options.text: The useful energy plus wasted energy equals less than 1000 J

options.label: B
options.text: The useful energy plus wasted energy equals exactly 1000 J

options.label: D
options.text: Only the useful energy comes from the electricity

explanation: According to conservation of energy, all the electrical energy (1000 J) is accounted for—it is either transferred usefully (heating water) or wasted (heating the kettle). The total is always conserved; nothing is lost or created.
